code: from cs1graphics import * from time import sleep, time paper = Canvas() paper.setBackgroundColor('skyBlue') paper.setWidth(800) paper.setHeight(500) paper.setTitle("My Dog") ground = Rectangle(800, 300, Point(400,350)) ground.setDepth(70) ground.setFillColor((0, 255, 0)) ground.setBorderColor((0, 255, 0)) paper.add(ground) house = Rectangle(100, 100, Point(700,200)) house.setFillColor((200,200,200)) paper.add(house) roof = Polygon(Point(770,150), Point(630, 150), Point(700,100)) roof.setDepth(30) roof.setFillColor((255, 0, 0)) paper.add(roof) def draw_animal(): global dog dog = Layer() body = Rectangle(100, 50, Point(600,300)) body.setDepth(10) body.setFillColor((195,111,49)) dog.add(body) leg1 = Rectangle(10, 50, Point(560,340)) leg1.setDepth(20) leg1.setFillColor((195,111,49)) dog.add(leg1) leg2 = Rectangle(10, 50, Point(580,340)) leg2.setDepth(20) leg2.setFillColor((195,111,49)) dog.add(leg2) leg3 = Rectangle(10, 50, Point(620,340)) leg3.setDepth(20) leg3.setFillColor((195,111,49)) dog.add(leg3) leg4 = Rectangle(10, 50, Point(640,340)) leg4.setDepth(20) leg4.setFillColor((195,111,49)) dog.add(leg4) global tail tail = Rectangle(10, 50, Point(650, 270)) tail.setDepth(20) tail.setFillColor((195,111,49)) tail.rotate(30) dog.add(tail) head = Circle(30, Point(550, 270)) head.setDepth(10) head.setFillColor((195,111,49)) dog.add(head) lefteye = Circle(5, Point(540, 260)) lefteye.setDepth(5) lefteye.setFillColor((0,0,0)) dog.add(lefteye) righteye = Circle(5, Point(560, 260)) righteye.setDepth(5) righteye.setFillColor((0,0,0)) dog.add(righteye) nose = Circle(3, Point(550,270)) nose.setDepth(5) nose.setFillColor((0,0,0)) dog.add(nose) paper.add(dog) def show_animation(): starttime = time() dif = 0 mode = "add" while time() < starttime + 6: dog.move(-10, 0) tail.rotate(30 + dif) if mode == "add": dif += 1 elif mode == "subtract": dif -= 1 if dif > 5: mode = "subtract" elif dif < -5: mode = "add" sleep(0.1) pass draw_animal() show_animation()

The list 'deck' have to include Card objects A Card is represented by a object with four attributes: the face, the suit, value, state, and the image object First, Have to define class 'Card' """ card_deck = [] for suitname in suit_names: for facename in face_names: card = Card() card.suit = suitname card.face = facename card.value = value[face_names.index(facename)] card.image = "%s%s_%s.png" % (img_path, suitname, facename) card_deck.append(card) random.shuffle(card_deck) return card_deck def hand_value(hand): """ hand is a list including card objects Compute the value of the cards in the list "hand" """ value = 0 for card in hand: value += card.value return value def card_string(card): """ Parameter "card" is a Card object Return a nice string to represent a card (sucn as "a King of Spades" or "an Ace of Diamonds") """ article = "a" if card.face == "Ace" or card.face == "8": article = "an" phrase = "%s %s of %s" % (article, card.face, card.suit) return phrase def ask_yesno(prompt): """ Display the text prompt and let's the user enter a string. If the user enters "y", the function returns "True", and if the user enters "n", the function returns "False". If the user enters anything else, the function prints "I beg your pardon!", and asks again, repreting this until the user has entered a correct string. """ answer = None while not (answer == "y" or answer == "n"): answer = input(prompt) print("I beg your pardon!") if answer == "y": return True elif answer == "n": return False def draw_card(dealer,player): """ This function add the cards of dealer and player to canvas, bj_board. If the state of each Card object is false, then you have to show the hidden card image(Back.png). The dealer's first card is hidden state. The parameter dealer and player are List objects including Card Objects. The start position of dealer's card is (100,100). The start position of player's card is (100,300). You can use the following methods for positioning images and text: Image() Object, Text() Object, moveTo() method, setDepth() method. You should use help function - help('cs1graphics.Image') -> about Image(), moveTo(), setDepth() help('cs1graphics.Text') -> about Text(),moveTo(), setDepth() """ depth = 100 x0,y0 = 100,100 x1,y1 = 100,300 bj_board.clear() def main(): deck = [] while True: # prompt for starting a new game and create a deck print ("Welcome to Black Jack 101!
") if len(deck) < 12: deck = create_deck() # create two hands of dealer and player dealer = [] player = [] # initial two dealings card = deck.pop() print ("You are dealt " + card_string(card)) player.append(card) card = deck.pop() print ("Dealer is dealt a hidden card") card.state=False dealer.append(card) card = deck.pop() print ("You are dealt " + card_string(card)) player.append(card) card = deck.pop() print ("Dealer is dealt " + card_string(card)) dealer.append(card) print ("Your total is", hand_value(player)) draw_card(dealer,player) # player's turn to draw cards while hand_value(player) < 21 and ask_yesno("Would you like another card? (y/n) "): # draw a card for the player card = deck.pop() print ("You are dealt " + card_string(card)) player.append(card) print ("Your total is", hand_value(player)) draw_card(dealer,player) # if the player's score is over 21, the player loses immediately. if hand_value(player) > 21: print ("You went over 21! You lost.") dealer[0].state = True draw_card(dealer,player) else: # draw cards for the dealer while the dealer's score is less than 17 print ("
The dealer's hidden card was " + card_string(dealer[0])) while hand_value(dealer) < 17: card = deck.pop() print ("Dealer is dealt " + card_string(card)) dealer.append(card) print ("The dealer's total is", hand_value(dealer)) dealer[0].state = True draw_card(dealer,player) # summary player_total = hand_value(player) dealer_total = hand_value(dealer) print ("
Your total is", player_total) print ("The dealer's total is", dealer_total) if dealer_total > 21: print ("The dealer went over 21! You win!") else: if player_total > dealer_total: print ("You win!") elif player_total < dealer_total: print ("You lost!") else: print ("You have a tie!") if not ask_yesno("
Play another round? (y/n) "): bj_board.close() break main()

code: import math #--------------------------------------------------------------# # # Function dec_to_any # # Input: # n: number to convert # radix: radix to use # # Output: # new_num: converted number # #--------------------------------------------------------------# def dec_to_any_list(n,radix): converted_number=[] n1=n while n>=radix: converted_number.append(n%radix) n=n//radix converted_number.append(n) converted_number.reverse() new_num=str() for i in range(len(converted_number)): if 0<=converted_number[i]<=9: converted_number[i]=str(converted_number[i]) elif converted_number[i]==10: converted_number[i]='A' elif converted_number[i]==11: converted_number[i]='B' elif converted_number[i]==12: converted_number[i]='C' elif converted_number[i]==13: converted_number[i]='D' elif converted_number[i]==14: converted_number[i]='E' elif converted_number[i]==15: converted_number[i]='F' new_num=new_num+new_num.join(converted_number) return new_num def dec_to_any_string(n, radix): converted_number="" n1=0 while n>=radix: if 0<=n%radix<=9: converted_number=str(n%radix)+converted_number elif n%radix==10: converted_number='A'+converted_number elif n%radix==11: converted_number='B'+converted_number elif n%radix==12: converted_number='C'+converted_number elif n%radix==13: converted_number='D'+converted_number elif n%radix==14: converted_number='E'+converted_number elif n%radix==15: converted_number='F'+converted_number n=n//radix if 0<=n<=9: converted_number=str(n)+converted_number elif n==10: converted_number='A'+converted_number elif n==11: converted_number='B'+converted_number elif n==12: converted_number='C'+converted_number elif n==13: converted_number='D'+converted_number elif n==14: converted_number='E'+converted_number elif n==15: converted_number='F'+converted_number new_num=converted_number return new_num; #--------------------------------------------------------------# # # Function main # # This is the driver function # Do commandline input/output and formatting here # # Example: # Enter a number: 61 # Enter a radix: 16 # 61 in base 10 is 3D in base 16 # # # Just digits, no gap or another character # 123 (O) ‘1’ ‘2’ ‘3’ (x) # “Wrong input!!!” if inputs are not in range # You must use the formatting operator % (TAs will check!!!) #--------------------------------------------------------------# def main(): while True: n=int(input("Enter a number: ")) if not n>0: print("Wrong Input!!!") continue radix=int(input("Enter a radix: ")) if not 2<=radix<=16: print("Wrong Input!!!") continue print("%d in base 10 is %s in base %d"%(n,dec_to_any_list(n,radix),radix)) print("%d in base 10 is %s in base %d"%(n,dec_to_any_string(n, radix),radix)) main()

The list 'deck' have to include Card objects A Card is represented by a object with four attributes: the face, the suit, value, state, and the image object First, Have to define class 'Card' """ def hand_value(hand): total=0 for card in hand: total += card.value() return total """ hand is a list including card objects Compute the value of the cards in the list "hand" """ def card_string(card): article="a " if card.face in [8, "Ace"]: article="an " return article + str(card.face) + " of " + card.suit """ Parameter "card" is a Card object Return a nice string to represent a card (sucn as "a King of Spades" or "an Ace of Diamonds") """ def ask_yesno(prompt): if prompt=='y': return True elif prompt=='n': return False """ Display the text prompt and let's the user enter a string. If the user enters "y", the function returns "True", and if the user enters "n", the function returns "False". If the user enters anything else, the function prints "I beg your pardon!", and asks again, repreting this until the user has entered a correct string. """ def draw_card(dealer,player): """ This funuction add the cards of dealer and player to canvas, bj_board. If the state of each Card object is false, then you have to show the hidden card image(Back.png). The dealer's first card is hidden state. The parameter dealer and player are List objects including Card Objects. The start position of dealer's card is (100,100). The start position of player's card is (100,300). You can use the following methods for positioning images and text: Image() Object, Text() Object, moveTo() method, setDepth() method. You should use help function - help('cs1graphics.Image') -> about Image(), moveTo(), setDepth() help('cs1graphics.Text') -> about Text(),moveTo(), setDepth() """ depth = 100 x0,y0 = 100,100 x1,y1 = 100,300 s=0 t=0 for card0 in dealer: img=card0.image() img.moveTo(x0+s, y0) img.setDepth(depth-s) bj_board.add(img) s+=20 for card1 in palyer: img=card1.image() img.moveTo(x1+t, y1) img.setDepth(depth-t) bj_board.add(img) t+=20 bj_board.clear() def main(): deck = [] while True: # prompt for starting a new game and create a deck print ("Welcome to Black Jack 101!
") if len(deck) < 12: deck = create_deck() # create two hands of dealer and player dealer = [] player = [] # initial two dealings card = deck.pop() print ("You are dealt " + card_string(card)) player.append(card) card = deck.pop() print ("Dealer is dealt a hidden card") card.state=False dealer.append(card) card = deck.pop() print ("You are dealt " + card_string(card)) player.append(card) card = deck.pop() print ("Dealer is dealt " + card_string(card)) dealer.append(card) print ("Your total is", hand_value(player)) draw_card(dealer,player) # player's turn to draw cards while hand_value(player) < 21 and ask_yesno("Would you like another card? (y/n) "): # draw a card for the player card = deck.pop() print ("You are dealt " + card_string(card)) player.append(card) print ("Your total is", hand_value(player)) draw_card(dealer,player) # if the player's score is over 21, the player loses immediately. if hand_value(player) > 21: print ("You went over 21! You lost.") dealer[0].state = True draw_card(dealer,player) else: # draw cards for the dealer while the dealer's score is less than 17 print ("
The dealer's hidden card was " + card_string(dealer[0])) while hand_value(dealer) < 17: card = deck.pop() print ("Dealer is dealt " + card_string(card)) dealer.append(card) print ("The dealer's total is", hand_value(dealer)) dealer[0].state = True draw_card(dealer,player) # summary player_total = hand_value(player) dealer_total = hand_value(dealer) print ("
Your total is", player_total) print ("The dealer's total is", dealer_total) if dealer_total > 21: print ("The dealer went over 21! You win!") else: if player_total > dealer_total: print ("You win!") elif player_total < dealer_total: print ("You lost!") else: print ("You have a tie!") if not ask_yesno("
Play another round? (y/n) "): bj_board.close() break main()

import random from cs1graphics import * img_path = './images/' suit_names = ['Clubs', 'Diamonds', 'Hearts', 'Spades'] face_names = ['Ace', '2', '3', '4', '5', '6', '7', '8', '9', '10', 'Jack', 'Queen', 'King'] value = [11, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 10, 10, 10] bj_board = Canvas(600, 400, 'dark green', 'Black Jack 101') print(img_path) """ Define the Card class """ class Card: """The Blackjack game""" def __init__(self, face, suit): assert face in face_names and suit in suit_names self.face = face self.suit = suit self.state = 'True' self.img = Image(img_path+self.suit+ '_' + self.face+'.png') def value(self): if len(self.face) == 1: return int(self.face) elif self.face == 'Ace': return 11 else: return 10 def __str__(self): article = 'a' if self.face in [8, "Ace"]: article = 'an' return (article + ' ' + str(self.face) + ' ' + 'of' + ' ' + self.suit) def create_deck(number = 1): """ Create a list("deck") of all 52 cards, shuffle them and return the list. The list 'deck' have to include Card objects A Card is represented by a object with four attributes: the face, the suit, value, state, and the image object First, Have to define class 'Card' """ deck = [] for face in face_names: for suit in suit_names: deck.append(Card(face, suit)) random.shuffle(deck) return deck def hand_value(hand): """ hand is a list including card objects Compute the value of the cards in the list "hand" """ total = 0 for card in hand: total += card.value() return total def card_string(card): """ Parameter "card" is a Card object Return a nice string to represent a card (sucn as "a King of Spades" or "an Ace of Diamonds") """ article = 'a' if card.face in [8, "Ace"]: article = 'an' return (article + ' ' + str(card.face) + ' ' + 'of' + ' ' + card.suit) def ask_yesno(prompt): """ Display the text prompt and let's the user enter a string. If the user enters "y", the function returns "True", and if the user enters "n", the function returns "False". If the user enters anything else, the function prints "I beg your pardon!", and asks again, repreting this until the user has entered a correct string. """ a = input(prompt) if a == 'y': return True elif a == 'n': return False else: print('I beg your pardon!') while True: a = input(prompt) if a == 'y': return True break elif a == 'n': return False break def draw_card(dealer,player): """ This funuction add the cards of dealer and player to canvas, bj_board. If the state of each Card object is false, then you have to show the hidden card image(Back.png). The dealer's first card is hidden state. The parameter dealer and player are List objects including Card Objects. The start position of dealer's card is (100,100). The start position of player's card is (100,300). You can use the following methods for positioning images and text: Image() Object, Text() Object, moveTo() method, setDepth() method. You should use help function - help('cs1graphics.Image') -> about Image(), moveTo(), setDepth() help('cs1graphics.Text') -> about Text(),moveTo(), setDepth() """ depth = 100 x0,y0 = 100,100 x1,y1 = 100,300 bj_board.clear() for i in range(len(player)): player[i].img.moveTo(100 + 15*i,300) bj_board.add(player[i].img) for i in range(len(dealer)): if dealer[i].state == 'False': dealer[i].img = Image(img_path + str('Back.png')) dealer[i].img.moveTo(100 + 15*i,100) bj_board.add(dealer[i].img) else: dealer[i].img.moveTo(100 + 15*i,100) bj_board.add(dealer[i].img) def main(): deck = [] while True: # prompt for starting a new game and create a deck print ("Welcome to Black Jack 101!
") if len(deck) < 12: deck = create_deck() # create two hands of dealer and player dealer = [] player = [] # initial two dealings card = deck.pop() print ("You are dealt " + card_string(card)) player.append(card) card = deck.pop() print ("Dealer is dealt a hidden card") card.state = 'False' dealer.append(card) card = deck.pop() print ("You are dealt " + card_string(card)) player.append(card) card = deck.pop() print ("Dealer is dealt " + card_string(card)) dealer.append(card) print ("Your total is", hand_value(player)) draw_card(dealer,player) # player's turn to draw cards while hand_value(player) < 21 and ask_yesno("Would you like another card? (y/n) "): # draw a card for the player card = deck.pop() print ("You are dealt " + card_string(card)) player.append(card) print ("Your total is", hand_value(player)) draw_card(dealer,player) # if the player's score is over 21, the player loses immediately. if hand_value(player) > 21: print ("You went over 21! You lost.") dealer[0].state = 'True' bj_board.remove(dealer[0].img) draw_card(dealer,player) else: # draw cards for the dealer while the dealer's score is less than 17 print ("
The dealer's hidden card was " + card_string(dealer[0])) while hand_value(dealer) < 17: card = deck.pop() print ("Dealer is dealt " + card_string(card)) dealer.append(card) print ("The dealer's total is", hand_value(dealer)) dealer[0].state = 'True' bj_board.remove(dealer[0].img) draw_card(dealer,player) # summary player_total = hand_value(player) dealer_total = hand_value(dealer) print ("
Your total is", player_total) print ("The dealer's total is", dealer_total) if dealer_total > 21: print ("The dealer went over 21! You win!") else: if player_total > dealer_total: print ("You win!") elif player_total < dealer_total: print ("You lost!") else: print ("You have a tie!") if not ask_yesno("
Play another round? (y/n) "): bj_board.close() break main()

import random from cs1graphics import * img_path = './images/' suit_names = ['Clubs', 'Diamonds', 'Hearts', 'Spades'] face_names = ['Ace', '2', '3', '4', '5', '6', '7', '8', '9', '10', 'Jack', 'Queen', 'King'] value = [11, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 10, 10, 10] decks=[] bj_board = Canvas(600, 400, 'dark green', 'Black Jack 101') """ Define the Card class """ class Card(): def __init__(self,suit,face,value,img,state): self.suit=suit self.face=face self.value=value self.img=img self.hidden=state def create_deck(number = 1): for i in range(4): for k in range(13): temp=Card(suit_names[i],face_names[k],value[k],img_path+suit_names[i]+'_'+face_names[k],False) # temp.__init__() random.shuffle(decks) return decks """ Create a list("deck") of all 52 cards, shuffle them and return the list. The list 'deck' have to include Card objects A Card is represented by a object with four attributes: the face, the suit, value, state, and the image object First, Have to define class 'Card' """ def hand_value(hand): """ hand is a list including card objects Compute the value of the cards in the list "hand" """ sum=0 for i in range(len(hand)): sum+=hand[i] return sum def card_string(card): """ Parameter "card" is a Card object Return a nice string to represent a card (sucn as "a King of Spades" or "an Ace of Diamonds") """ a=card.face+" of "+card.suit return a def ask_yesno(prompt): """ Display the text prompt and let's the user enter a string. If the user enters "y", the function returns "True", and if the user enters "n", the function returns "False". If the user enters anything else, the function prints "I beg your pardon!", and asks again, repreting this until the user has entered a correct string. """ a=input() if a=='y': return True elif a=='n': return False else: print("I beg your pardon!") return ask_yesno("Would you like another card? (y/n) ") def draw_card(dealer,player): """ This funuction add the cards of dealer and player to canvas, bj_board. If the state of each Card object is false, then you have to show the hidden card image(Back.png). The dealer's first card is hidden state. The parameter dealer and player are List objects including Card Objects. The start position of dealer's card is (100,100). The start position of player's card is (100,300). You can use the following methods for positioning images and text: Image() Object, Text() Object, moveTo() method, setDepth() method. You should use help function - help('cs1graphics.Image') -> about Image(), moveTo(), setDepth() help('cs1graphics.Text') -> about Text(),moveTo(), setDepth() """ depth = 100 x0,y0 = 100,100 x1,y1 = 100,300 x=0 a= for i in range(len(dealer)): if dealer[i].hidden==False: a.moveTo(x0+x,y0) a.img.setDepth(depth*i) x+=10 bj_board.add(a) elif dealer[i].hidden==True: dealer[i].img.moveTo(x0+x,y0) dealer[i].img.setDepth(depth*i) x+=10 bj_board.add(dealer[i].img) bj_board.clear() def main(): deck = [] while True: # prompt for starting a new game and create a deck print ("Welcome to Black Jack 101!
") if len(deck) < 12: deck = create_deck() # create two hands of dealer and player dealer = [] player = [] # initial two dealings card = deck.pop() print ("You are dealt " + card_string(card)) player.append(card) card = deck.pop() print ("Dealer is dealt a hidden card") card.state=False dealer.append(card) card = deck.pop() print ("You are dealt " + card_string(card)) player.append(card) card = deck.pop() print ("Dealer is dealt " + card_string(card)) dealer.append(card) print ("Your total is", hand_value(player)) draw_card(dealer,player) # player's turn to draw cards while hand_value(player) < 21 and ask_yesno("Would you like another card? (y/n) "): # draw a card for the player card = deck.pop() print ("You are dealt " + card_string(card)) player.append(card) print ("Your total is", hand_value(player)) draw_card(dealer,player) # if the player's score is over 21, the player loses immediately. if hand_value(player) > 21: print ("You went over 21! You lost.") dealer[0].state = True draw_card(dealer,player) else: # draw cards for the dealer while the dealer's score is less than 17 print ("
The dealer's hidden card was " + card_string(dealer[0])) while hand_value(dealer) < 17: card = deck.pop() print ("Dealer is dealt " + card_string(card)) dealer.append(card) print ("The dealer's total is", hand_value(dealer)) dealer[0].state = True draw_card(dealer,player) # summary player_total = hand_value(player) dealer_total = hand_value(dealer) print ("
Your total is", player_total) print ("The dealer's total is", dealer_total) if dealer_total > 21: print ("The dealer went over 21! You win!") else: if player_total > dealer_total: print ("You win!") elif player_total < dealer_total: print ("You lost!") else: print ("You have a tie!") if not ask_yesno("
Play another round? (y/n) "): bj_board.close() break main()

code: from math import * from cs1media import * img = load_picture( "gurumi.png" ) w,h = img.size() #new_w,new_h = #----------------------------------------------------------# # # Function skew # # Input: # img: loaded image # direction: vertical or horizontal # angle: -89 to 89 degrees # # Output: # new_img: skewed image # print “Wrong input!!!” if inputs are not in range # #----------------------------------------------------------# def skew(img, direction, angle): #implement here w,h=img.size() if int(angle)<-89 or int(angle)>89: print("Wrong input!!!") return t=tan(radians(int(angle))) if direction=='vertical': if t>=0: h1=h+w*t new_img=create_picture(w,h1,black) for x in range(w): for y in range(h): r,g,b=img.get(x,y) new_img.set(x,y+x*t,(r,g,b)) else: h1=h-w*t new_img=create_picture(w,h1,black) for x in range(w): for y in range(h): r,g,b=img.get(x,y) new_img.set(x,y+(x-w)*t,(r,g,b)) else: #horizontal if t>=0: w1=w+h*t new_img=create_picture(w1,h,black) for x in range(w): for y in range(h): r,g,b=img.get(x,y) new_img.set(x+y*t,y,(r,g,b)) else: w1=w-h*t new_img=create_picture(w1,h,black) for x in range(w): for y in range(h): r,g,b=img.get(x,y) new_img.set(x+(y-h)*t,y,(r,g,b)) new_img.show()

code: import math #--------------------------------------------------------------# # # Function dec_to_any # # Input: # n: number to convert # radix: radix to use # # Output: # new_num: converted number # #--------------------------------------------------------------# def dec_to_any_list(n,radix): new_num = [] n = int(n) r = int(radix) while n >= r: b = n%r b = str(b) new_num.insert(0, b) n = n // r if n < r: n = str(n) new_num.insert(0, n) return new_num; def dec_to_any_string(n, radix): a = [] n = int(n) r = int(radix) while n >= r: b = n%r b = str(b) a.insert(0, b) n = n // r if n < r: n = str(n) a.insert(0, n) new_num = ''.join(a) return new_num; #--------------------------------------------------------------# # # Function main # # This is the driver function # Do commandline input/output and formatting here # # Example: # Enter a number: 61 # Enter a radix: 16 # 61 in base 10 is 3D in base 16 # # # Just digits, no gap or another character # 123 (O) ‘1’ ‘2’ ‘3’ (x) # “Wrong input!!!” if inputs are not in range # You must use the formatting operator % (TAs will check!!!) #--------------------------------------------------------------# def main(): n = input("Enter a number: ") radix = input("Enter a radix: ") n = int(n) r = int(radix) if n < 0 or r < 2 or r > 16: print("Wrong Input!!!") else: n1 = dec_to_any_list(n, radix) n2 = dec_to_any_string(n, radix) n3 = ''.join(n1) if n3 == n2: new_num = n2 print("%s in base 10 is %s in base %s." %(n, new_num, radix)) main()

import random from cs1graphics import * img_path = './images/' suit_names = ['Clubs', 'Diamonds', 'Hearts', 'Spades'] face_names = ['Ace', '2', '3', '4', '5', '6', '7', '8', '9', '10', 'Jack', 'Queen', 'King'] value = [11, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 10, 10, 10] bj_board = Canvas(600, 400, 'dark green', 'Black Jack 101') print(img_path) """ Define the Card class """ class Card: """The Blackjack game""" def __init__(self, face, suit): assert face in face_names and suit in suit_names self.face = face self.suit = suit self.state = 'True' if self.state == 'True': self.img = Image(img_path+self.suit+ '_' + self.face+'.png') else: self.img = Image(img_path+str(Back.png)) def value(self): if len(self.face) == 1: return int(self.face) elif self.face == 'Ace': return 11 else: return 10 def __str__(self): article = 'a' if self.face in [8, "Ace"]: article = 'an' return (article + ' ' + str(self.face) + ' ' + 'of' + ' ' + self.suit) def create_deck(number = 1): """ Create a list("deck") of all 52 cards, shuffle them and return the list. The list 'deck' have to include Card objects A Card is represented by a object with four attributes: the face, the suit, value, state, and the image object First, Have to define class 'Card' """ deck = [] for face in face_names: for suit in suit_names: deck.append(Card(face, suit)) random.shuffle(deck) return deck def hand_value(hand): """ hand is a list including card objects Compute the value of the cards in the list "hand" """ total = 0 for card in hand: total += card.value() return total def card_string(card): """ Parameter "card" is a Card object Return a nice string to represent a card (sucn as "a King of Spades" or "an Ace of Diamonds") """ article = 'a' if card.face in [8, "Ace"]: article = 'an' return (article + ' ' + str(card.face) + ' ' + 'of' + ' ' + card.suit) def ask_yesno(prompt): """ Display the text prompt and let's the user enter a string. If the user enters "y", the function returns "True", and if the user enters "n", the function returns "False". If the user enters anything else, the function prints "I beg your pardon!", and asks again, repreting this until the user has entered a correct string. """ a = input(prompt) if a == 'y': return True elif a == 'n': return False else: print('I beg your pardon!') def draw_card(dealer,player): """ This funuction add the cards of dealer and player to canvas, bj_board. ") if len(deck) < 12: deck = create_deck() # create two hands of dealer and player dealer = [] player = [] # initial two dealings card = deck.pop() print ("You are dealt " + card_string(card)) player.append(card) card = deck.pop() print ("Dealer is dealt a hidden card") card.state = 'False' dealer.append(card) card = deck.pop() print ("You are dealt " + card_string(card)) player.append(card) card = deck.pop() print ("Dealer is dealt " + card_string(card)) dealer.append(card) print ("Your total is", hand_value(player)) draw_card(dealer,player) # player's turn to draw cards while hand_value(player) < 21 and ask_yesno("Would you like another card? (y/n) "): # draw a card for the player card = deck.pop() print ("You are dealt " + card_string(card)) player.append(card) print ("Your total is", hand_value(player)) draw_card(dealer,player) # if the player's score is over 21, the player loses immediately. if hand_value(player) > 21: print ("You went over 21! You lost.") dealer[0].state = True draw_card(dealer,player) else: # draw cards for the dealer while the dealer's score is less than 17 print ("
The dealer's hidden card was " + card_string(dealer[0])) while hand_value(dealer) < 17: card = deck.pop() print ("Dealer is dealt " + card_string(card)) dealer.append(card) print ("The dealer's total is", hand_value(dealer)) dealer[0].state = True draw_card(dealer,player) # summary player_total = hand_value(player) dealer_total = hand_value(dealer) print ("
Your total is", player_total) print ("The dealer's total is", dealer_total) if dealer_total > 21: print ("The dealer went over 21! You win!") else: if player_total > dealer_total: print ("You win!") elif player_total < dealer_total: print ("You lost!") else: print ("You have a tie!") if not ask_yesno("
Play another round? (y/n) "): bj_board.close() break main()
